# Tae Kim Grammar Guide (Split Index)

This index lists each lesson file for retrieval and chunked loading.

## Before you start

- 001 Before you begin -> before-you-start/001-before-you-begin.md
- 002 Other formats -> before-you-start/002-other-formats.md
- 003 The problem with conventional textbooks -> before-you-start/003-the-problem-with-conventional-textbooks.md
- 004 A Japanese guide to learning Japanese grammar -> before-you-start/004-a-japanese-guide-to-learning-japanese-grammar.md
- 005 Suggestions -> before-you-start/005-suggestions.md

## Basic Grammatical Structures

### Expressing state-of-being

- 019 Declaring something is so and so using 「だ」 -> basic-grammatical-structures/expressing-state-of-being/019-declaring-something-is-so-and-so-using.md
- 020 Conjugating to the negative state-of-being -> basic-grammatical-structures/expressing-state-of-being/020-conjugating-to-the-negative-state-of-being.md
- 021 Conjugating to the past state-of-being -> basic-grammatical-structures/expressing-state-of-being/021-conjugating-to-the-past-state-of-being.md
- 022 Conjugation summary -> basic-grammatical-structures/expressing-state-of-being/022-conjugation-summary.md

- 023 Defining grammatical functions with particles -> basic-grammatical-structures/introduction-to-particles/023-defining-grammatical-functions-with-particles.md
- 024 The 「は」 topic particle -> basic-grammatical-structures/introduction-to-particles/024-the-topic-particle.md
- 025 The 「も」 inclusive topic particle -> basic-grammatical-structures/introduction-to-particles/025-the-inclusive-topic-particle.md
- 026 The 「が」 identifier particle -> basic-grammatical-structures/introduction-to-particles/026-the-identifier-particle.md

- 034 Negative Verbs -> basic-grammatical-structures/negative-verbs/034-negative-verbs.md

### Past Tense

- 036 Past tense for る-verbs -> basic-grammatical-structures/past-tense/036-past-tense-for-verbs.md
- 037 Past tense for う-verbs -> basic-grammatical-structures/past-tense/037-past-tense-for-verbs.md
- 038 Past-negative tense for all verbs -> basic-grammatical-structures/past-tense/038-past-negative-tense-for-all-verbs.md
### Particles used with verbs

- 039 The direct object 「を」 particle -> basic-grammatical-structures/particles-used-with-verbs/039-the-direct-object-particle.md
- 040 The target 「に」 particle -> basic-grammatical-structures/particles-used-with-verbs/040-the-target-particle.md
- 041 The directional 「へ」 particle -> basic-grammatical-structures/particles-used-with-verbs/041-the-directional-particle.md
- 042 The contextual 「で」 particle -> basic-grammatical-structures/particles-used-with-verbs/042-the-contextual-particle.md
- 043 When location is the topic -> basic-grammatical-structures/particles-used-with-verbs/043-when-location-is-the-topic.md
- 044 When direct object is the topic -> basic-grammatical-structures/particles-used-with-verbs/044-when-direct-object-is-the-topic.md
### Transitive and Intransitive Verbs

- 045 Transitive and Intransitive Verbs -> basic-grammatical-structures/transitive-and-intransitive-verbs/045-transitive-and-intransitive-verbs.md

### Relative Clauses and Sentence Order

- 047 Treating verbs and state-of-being like adjectives -> basic-grammatical-structures/relative-clauses-and-sentence-order/047-treating-verbs-and-state-of-being-like-adjectives.md

- 050 Japanese Sentence Order -> basic-grammatical-structures/relative-clauses-and-sentence-order/050-japanese-sentence-order.md

- 051 The last three particles (Not!) -> basic-grammatical-structures/noun-related-particles/051-the-last-three-particles-not.md

- 053 The Vague Listing 「や」 and 「とか」 particles -> basic-grammatical-structures/noun-related-particles/053-the-vague-listing-and-particles.md

- 060 Combining both to get 「よね」 -> basic-grammatical-structures/adverbs-and-sentence-ending-particles/060-combining-both-to-get.md
## Essential Grammar

### Polite Form and Verb Stems

- 061 Not being rude in Japan -> essential-grammar/polite-form-and-verb-stems/061-not-being-rude-in-japan.md
- 062 The stem of verbs -> essential-grammar/polite-form-and-verb-stems/062-the-stem-of-verbs.md
- 063 Using 「～ます」 to make verbs polite -> essential-grammar/polite-form-and-verb-stems/063-using-to-make-verbs-polite.md
- 064 Using 「です」 for everything else -> essential-grammar/polite-form-and-verb-stems/064-using-for-everything-else.md
- 065 「です」 is NOT the same as 「だ」 -> essential-grammar/polite-form-and-verb-stems/065-is-not-the-same-as.md

- 066 Referring to yourself -> essential-grammar/addressing-people/066-referring-to-yourself.md

- 069 Referring to others in third person -> essential-grammar/addressing-people/069-referring-to-others-in-third-person.md
- 070 Referring to family members -> essential-grammar/addressing-people/070-referring-to-family-members.md

- 071 Questions in polite form -> essential-grammar/the-question-marker/071-questions-in-polite-form.md

- 074 Using question words -> essential-grammar/the-question-marker/074-using-question-words.md
### Compound Sentences

- 075 Expressing a sequence of states -> essential-grammar/compound-sentences/075-expressing-a-sequence-of-states.md
- 076 Expressing a sequence of verbs with the て-form -> essential-grammar/compound-sentences/076-expressing-a-sequence-of-verbs-with-the-form.md
- 077 Expressing reason or causation using 「から」 and 「ので」 -> essential-grammar/compound-sentences/077-expressing-reason-or-causation-using-and.md
- 078 Using 「のに」 to mean 'despite' -> essential-grammar/compound-sentences/078-using-to-mean-despite.md
- 079 Expressing contradiction using 「が」 and 「けど」 -> essential-grammar/compound-sentences/079-expressing-contradiction-using-and.md
- 080 Expressing multiple reasons using 「し」 -> essential-grammar/compound-sentences/080-expressing-multiple-reasons-using.md
- 081 Expressing multiple actions or states using 「～たりする」 -> essential-grammar/compound-sentences/081-expressing-multiple-actions-or-states-using.md
### Other uses of the て-form

- 082 Using 「～ている」 for enduring states -> essential-grammar/other-uses-of-the-form/082-using-for-enduring-states.md
- 083 Enduring state-of-being vs enduring state of action -> essential-grammar/other-uses-of-the-form/083-enduring-state-of-being-vs-enduring-state-of-action.md
- 084 Using 「～てある」 for resultant states -> essential-grammar/other-uses-of-the-form/084-using-for-resultant-states.md
- 085 Using the 「～ておく」 form as preparation for the future -> essential-grammar/other-uses-of-the-form/085-using-the-form-as-preparation-for-the-future.md
- 086 Using motion verbs (行く、来る) with the て-form -> essential-grammar/other-uses-of-the-form/086-using-motion-verbs-with-the-form.md
### Potential Form

- 087 Expressing the ability to do something -> essential-grammar/potential-form/087-expressing-the-ability-to-do-something.md
- 088 The Potential Form -> essential-grammar/potential-form/088-the-potential-form.md

- 091 「ある」, yet another exception -> essential-grammar/potential-form/091-yet-another-exception.md
### Using する and なる with the に particle

- 092 Using 「なる」 and 「する」 for nouns and な-adjectives -> essential-grammar/using-and-with-the-particle/092-using-and-for-nouns-and-adjectives.md
- 093 Using 「なる」 with い-adjectives -> essential-grammar/using-and-with-the-particle/093-using-with-adjectives.md
- 094 Using 「なる」 and 「する」 with verbs -> essential-grammar/using-and-with-the-particle/094-using-and-with-verbs.md
### Conditionals

- 095 How to say 'if' in Japanese -> essential-grammar/conditionals/095-how-to-say-if-in-japanese.md
- 096 Expressing natural consequence using 「と」 -> essential-grammar/conditionals/096-expressing-natural-consequence-using.md
- 097 Contextual conditionals using 「なら (ば) 」 -> essential-grammar/conditionals/097-contextual-conditionals-using.md
- 098 General conditionals using 「ば」 -> essential-grammar/conditionals/098-general-conditionals-using.md
- 099 Past conditional using 「たら (ば) 」 -> essential-grammar/conditionals/099-past-conditional-using.md
- 100 How does 「もし」 fit into all of this? -> essential-grammar/conditionals/100-how-does-fit-into-all-of-this.md
### Expressing "must" or "have to"

- 144 Showing contempt for an action with 「～やがる」 -> essential-grammar/casual-patterns-and-slang/144-showing-contempt-for-an-action-with.md

## Special Expressions

### Special Expressions with Generic Nouns

- 160 Using 「こと」 to say whether something has happened -> special-expressions/special-expressions-with-generic-nouns/160-using-to-say-whether-something-has-happened.md

- 162 Using 「もの」 as a casual feminine way to emphasize -> special-expressions/special-expressions-with-generic-nouns/162-using-as-a-casual-feminine-way-to-emphasize.md
### Various degrees of certainty

- 163 Using 「かもしれない」 to express uncertainty -> special-expressions/various-degrees-of-certainty/163-using-to-express-uncertainty.md
- 164 Using 「でしょう」 to express a fair amount of certainty (polite) -> special-expressions/various-degrees-of-certainty/164-using-to-express-a-fair-amount-of-certainty-polite.md
- 165 Using 「でしょう」 and 「だろう」 to express strong amount of certainty (casual) -> special-expressions/various-degrees-of-certainty/165-using-and-to-express-strong-amount-of-certainty-casual.md
### Expressing Amounts

- 166 Indicating that's all there is using 「だけ」 -> special-expressions/expressing-amounts/166-indicating-thats-all-there-is-using.md
- 167 Using 「のみ」 as a formal version of 「だけ」 -> special-expressions/expressing-amounts/167-using-as-a-formal-version-of.md
- 168 Indication that there's nothing else using 「しか」 -> special-expressions/expressing-amounts/168-indication-that-theres-nothing-else-using.md
- 169 Expressing the opposite of 「だけ」 with 「ばかり」 -> special-expressions/expressing-amounts/169-expressing-the-opposite-of-with.md
- 170 Saying there's too much of something using 「すぎる」 -> special-expressions/expressing-amounts/170-saying-theres-too-much-of-something-using.md
- 171 Adding the 「も」 particle to express excessive amounts -> special-expressions/expressing-amounts/171-adding-the-particle-to-express-excessive-amounts.md
- 172 Using 「ほど」 to express the extent of something -> special-expressions/expressing-amounts/172-using-to-express-the-extent-of-something.md
- 173 Using 「～さ」 with adjectives to indicate an amount -> special-expressions/expressing-amounts/173-using-with-adjectives-to-indicate-an-amount.md
### Similarity or hearsay

- 174 Expressing similarity with よう (様) -> special-expressions/similarity-or-hearsay/174-expressing-similarity-with.md
- 175 Using 「みたい」 to say something looks like something else -> special-expressions/similarity-or-hearsay/175-using-to-say-something-looks-like-something-else.md
- 176 Guessing at an outcome using 「～そう」 -> special-expressions/similarity-or-hearsay/176-guessing-at-an-outcome-using.md
- 177 Expressing hearsay using 「～そうだ」 -> special-expressions/similarity-or-hearsay/177-expressing-hearsay-using.md
- 178 Expressing hearsay or behavior using 「～らしい」 -> special-expressions/similarity-or-hearsay/178-expressing-hearsay-or-behavior-using.md
- 179 「っぽい」: Slang expression of similarity -> special-expressions/similarity-or-hearsay/179-slang-expression-of-similarity.md
### Using 「方」 and 「よる」

- 180 Using 「方」 for comparisons -> special-expressions/using-and/180-using-for-comparisons.md
- 181 Using 「より」 for comparisons -> special-expressions/using-and/181-using-for-comparisons.md
- 182 Using 「方」 to express a way to do something -> special-expressions/using-and/182-using-to-express-a-way-to-do-something.md
- 183 Using 「によって」 to express dependency -> special-expressions/using-and/183-using-to-express-dependency.md
- 184 Indicating a source of information using 「によると」 -> special-expressions/using-and/184-indicating-a-source-of-information-using.md
### Actions that are easy or hard to do

- 185 Actions that are easy or hard to do -> special-expressions/actions-that-are-easy-or-hard-to-do/185-actions-that-are-easy-or-hard-to-do.md
- 186 Variations of 「～にくい」 with 「～がたい」 and 「～づらい」 -> special-expressions/actions-that-are-easy-or-hard-to-do/186-variations-of-with-and.md
### More negative verbs

- 187 Doing something without doing something else -> special-expressions/more-negative-verbs/187-doing-something-without-doing-something-else.md
- 188 A casual masculine type of negative that ends in 「ん」 -> special-expressions/more-negative-verbs/188-a-casual-masculine-type-of-negative-that-ends-in.md
- 189 A classical negative verb that ends in 「ぬ」 -> special-expressions/more-negative-verbs/189-a-classical-negative-verb-that-ends-in.md

- 190 Coming to a conclusion with 「わけ」 -> special-expressions/hypothesizing-and-concluding/190-coming-to-a-conclusion-with.md
- 191 Making hypotheses with 「とする」 -> special-expressions/hypothesizing-and-concluding/191-making-hypotheses-with.md
### Time-specific actions

- 192 Expressing what just happened with 「～ばかり」 -> special-expressions/time-specific-actions/192-expressing-what-just-happened-with.md
- 193 Express what occurred immediately after with 「とたん」 -> special-expressions/time-specific-actions/193-express-what-occurred-immediately-after-with.md
- 194 Using 「ながら」 for two concurrent actions -> special-expressions/time-specific-actions/194-using-for-two-concurrent-actions.md
- 195 Using 「ながら」 with state-of-being -> special-expressions/time-specific-actions/195-using-with-state-of-being.md
- 196 To repeat something with reckless abandon using 「まくる」 -> special-expressions/time-specific-actions/196-to-repeat-something-with-reckless-abandon-using.md
### Expressing a lack of change

- 197 Using 「まま」 to express a lack of change -> special-expressions/expressing-a-lack-of-change/197-using-to-express-a-lack-of-change.md
- 198 Using 「っぱなし」 to leave something the way it is -> special-expressions/expressing-a-lack-of-change/198-using-to-leave-something-the-way-it-is.md
## Advanced Topics

### Formal Expressions

- 199 What do you mean by formal expressions? -> advanced-topics/formal-expressions/199-what-do-you-mean-by-formal-expressions.md
- 200 Using 「である」 for formal state-of-being -> advanced-topics/formal-expressions/200-using-for-formal-state-of-being.md
- 201 Negative of 「である」 -> advanced-topics/formal-expressions/201-negative-of.md
- 202 Sequential relative clauses in formal language -> advanced-topics/formal-expressions/202-sequential-relative-clauses-in-formal-language.md
### Things that should be a certain way

- 203 Using 「はず」 to describe an expectation -> advanced-topics/things-that-should-be-a-certain-way/203-using-to-describe-an-expectation.md
- 204 Using 「べき」 to describe actions one should do -> advanced-topics/things-that-should-be-a-certain-way/204-using-to-describe-actions-one-should-do.md
- 205 Using 「べく」 to describe what one tries to do -> advanced-topics/things-that-should-be-a-certain-way/205-using-to-describe-what-one-tries-to-do.md
- 206 Using 「べからず」 to describe things one must not do -> advanced-topics/things-that-should-be-a-certain-way/206-using-to-describe-things-one-must-not-do.md

- 207 Using 「 (で) さえ」 to describe the minimum requirement -> advanced-topics/the-minimum-expectation/207-using-to-describe-the-minimum-requirement.md
- 208 「 (で) すら」 – Older version of 「 (で) さえ」 -> advanced-topics/the-minimum-expectation/208-older-version-of.md
- 209 「おろか」 – It's not even worth considering -> advanced-topics/the-minimum-expectation/209-its-not-even-worth-considering.md

- 210 Showing outward signs of an emotion using 「～がる」 -> advanced-topics/showing-signs-of-something/210-showing-outward-signs-of-an-emotion-using.md

- 212 Using 「めく」 to indicate an atmosphere of a state -> advanced-topics/showing-signs-of-something/212-using-to-indicate-an-atmosphere-of-a-state.md

- 215 Expressing what cannot be done with 「～かねる」 -> advanced-topics/expressing-non-feasibility/215-expressing-what-cannot-be-done-with.md
